title: Delicate Malty Middle Eastern Beef Flank Steak Harees
description: A malty and velvety Middle Eastern dish featuring beef flank steak, served in a mason jar, perfect for a hangover cure. This ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly recipe is made with a mother-sauce-base and has a harees form.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the onion until it is translucent.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, salt, and black pepper to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the sliced beef flank steak to the pan and cook until it is browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the malt syrup to the pan and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a mason jar and bake in the preheated oven for 2 hours.
Remove the jar from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Serve the harees in the mason jar, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
